You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/modals/advanced-kms-modal/advanced-vault-modal.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 338, 343
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: secondary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

### Fix 2

Rule [family:Modal]:
## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:
  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
  title -> <ModalHeader />
  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  actions -> map to appropriate child component (any)
  bodyAriaLabel -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  bodyAriaRole -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  footer -> ModalFooter (as children, ReactNode)
  hasNoBodyWrapper -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  header -> ModalHeader (as children, ReactNode)
  showClose -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  titleLabel -> map to appropriate child component (string)

Removed children (no longer valid JSX): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Add imports: ModalBody, ModalHeader, ModalFooter from '@patternfly/react-core'

Remove imports (if no longer used): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

### Fix 3
Lines: 338, 341
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ModalBoxFooter

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 338):
  337        <ModalFooter>
  338          <Button key="cancel" variant="secondary" onClick={closeModal}>
  339            {t('Cancel')}

  (line 341):
  340          </Button>
  341          <Button
  342            key="Save"

```

### Fix 4
Line: 265
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  264              <HelperText>
  265                <HelperTextItem>
  266                  {t(

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/modals/advanced-kms-modal/advanced-vault-modal.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current state of the code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Button variant type change):** Lines 338 and 343 use `variant="secondary"` and `variant="primary"`. The type change adds `'stateful'` to the union but doesn't remove anything. Both `"secondary"` and `"primary"` are still valid values. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2 (Modal family migration):** The Modal at line 171 uses:
- `header={Header}` → needs to move to `<ModalHeader>` child
- `showClose={false}` → removed prop, remove it
- `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}` → removed prop, remove it

Looking at the code: Line 3 imports `ModalBody, ModalFooter, ModalHeader` from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` — these are the **shared** wrappers, not PF's. Line 10 imports `Modal` from `@patternfly/react-core`. The `Header` variable (lines 167-169) wraps text in the shared `<ModalHeader>`.

For PF v6 Modal migration:
1. Remove `header={Header}` prop from Modal
2. Remove `showClose={false}` prop
3. Remove `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}` prop  
4. Place the header content as a PF `<ModalHeader>` child inside the Modal
5. Need to import PF's `ModalHeader` — but there's a naming conflict with `@odf/shared/modals/Modal`'s `ModalHeader`

Let me look at what the shared `ModalHeader` does vs PF's:

The `Header` variable creates `<ModalHeader>{t('Key Management Service Advanced Settings')}</ModalHeader>` using the shared import. In PF v6, `<ModalHeader title="...">` is the pattern. But since the `header` prop value is a JSX element using the *shared* ModalHeader, and PF v6 needs its *own* ModalHeader as a child component, I need to:
- Add PF's `ModalHeader` as a child of `<Modal>` with `title` prop
- The `Header` variable and the shared `ModalHeader` import become unused for the Modal header (but I need to check if shared `ModalBody` and `ModalFooter` are still used)

Wait — looking more carefully at lines 180 and 337: `<ModalBody>` and `<ModalFooter>` are used. These come from the shared import (line 3). But in PF v6, Modal expects PF's own `ModalBody`, `ModalHeader`, `ModalFooter` as children.

The shared imports at line 3 are from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` — these are the project's own wrapper components, not PF components. The PF v6 migration needs PF's `ModalHeader`, `ModalBody`, `ModalFooter`.

Since the file already uses `ModalBody` and `ModalFooter` from the shared lib (and they're wrapping the content inside `<Modal>`), the PF v6 migration of the Modal component itself needs:
1. Remove `header`, `showClose`, `hasNoBodyWrapper` props
2. Place a PF `ModalHeader` as a child — but since there's a naming conflict, I'll use the PF `ModalHeader` with `title` prop. 

Actually, let me reconsider. The shared `ModalBody` and `ModalFooter` from `@odf/shared` are likely wrapper components specific to this project. The PF v6 Modal expects PF's own `ModalBody`/`ModalHeader`/`ModalFooter`. But I shouldn't change the shared imports since that's a different concern — I should focus on the Modal component's props.

The simplest correct approach:
1. Remove `header={Header}` from Modal, place a PF `ModalHeader` with `title` as first child
2. Remove `showClose={false}` and `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}`
3. Import PF's `ModalHeader` — rename to avoid conflict with shared one
4. The `Header` variable becomes unused — remove it

Since PF's `ModalHeader` conflicts with the shared import, I'll alias the PF import. But looking again at what's imported: line 3 imports `ModalBody, ModalFooter, ModalHeader` from shared. If I import PF's `ModalHeader` too, I need an alias.

Actually, looking at this more carefully: in PF v6, Modal children should be PF's `ModalHeader`, `ModalBody`, `ModalFooter`. The shared wrappers at line 3 might need to be replaced entirely. But the fix instructions say to focus on the Modal props migration. Let me just handle what's asked:

For the `header` prop: move to a PF `ModalHeader` child with `title` prop. Since the shared `ModalHeader` is imported on line 3, I'll need to distinguish them. The simplest approach is to alias the PF import: `ModalHeader as PfModalHeader` — but actually, let me check if the shared `ModalHeader` is used elsewhere.

The `Header` variable (line 168) uses `<ModalHeader>` from the shared import. If I remove the `Header` variable (since the `header` prop is being removed), the shared `ModalHeader` import may no longer be needed. Let me check... the shared `ModalBody` and `ModalFooter` ARE used (lines 180, 337). Only `ModalHeader` from the shared import is used in the `Header` variable. So if I remove `Header`, shared `ModalHeader` becomes unused.

Plan: Remove `ModalHeader` from the shared import (line 3), import PF's `ModalHeader` (line 6-16), remove the `Header` variable, and add `<ModalHeader title={t('Key Management Service Advanced Settings')} />` as first child of Modal.

**Fix 3 (Button icon children to prop):** Lines 338-340 and 341-349. Looking at the Button children:
- Line 338-340: `<Button key="cancel" variant="secondary" onClick={closeModal}>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — this is text only, no icon. No change needed.
- Line 341-349: `<Button key="Save" variant="primary" ...>{t('Save')}</Button>` — also text only, no icon. No change needed.

Both Buttons contain only text, no icon elements. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 4 (HelperTextItem icon):** Line 265: `<HelperTextItem>{t(...)}</HelperTextItem>` — this contains only text, no icon. **No code change needed.**

So the only real changes are from **Fix 2**. Let me apply them:
Now remove the `Header` variable (lines 167-169) since it will no longer be used:
Now update the Modal component: remove `header`, `showClose`, `hasNoBodyWrapper` props, and add `<ModalHeader>` as first child:

    >

      <ModalBody>

content.text: Now let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct. Let me verify all the changes are properly applied:

1. **Line 3**: `ModalHeader` removed from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` import (no longer used after removing the `Header` variable)
2. **Line 16**: `ModalHeader` added to `@patternfly/react-core` import (PF v6's ModalHeader)
3. **Lines 167-169** (old): `Header` variable removed (was only used by the removed `header` prop)
4. **Lines 169-174**: Modal props cleaned up — `header={Header}`, `showClose={false}`, `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}` all removed
5. **Line 175**: PF v6 `<ModalHeader title={...} />` added as first child of `<Modal>`
6. **Lines 334, 339**: `variant="secondary"` and `variant="primary"` — unchanged (still valid in v6)
7. **Lines 334-345**: Buttons contain only text, no icons — no icon migration needed
8. **Line 261**: `<HelperTextItem>` contains only text, no icon — no icon migration needed

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — Button variant type change
**No change needed.** The values `"secondary"` (line 334) and `"primary"` (line 339) are both still valid in the new type union. The only change was the addition of `"stateful"` — no existing values were removed.

### Fix 2 — Modal family migration
**Applied.** Three changes made:
1. **Removed `header={Header}` prop** from `<Modal>` and replaced with `<ModalHeader title={t('Key Management Service Advanced Settings')} />` as a direct child of `<Modal>` (PF v6 composition pattern).
2. **Removed `showClose={false}` prop** — no longer exists in PF v6 Modal API (close button is controlled by `onClose` presence on `<Modal>`).
3. **Removed `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}` prop** — no longer exists in PF v6 Modal API.
4. **Removed the `Header` variable** (was `const Header = <ModalHeader>...</ModalHeader>`) — now unused after removing the `header` prop.
5. **Updated imports**: Removed `ModalHeader` from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` (no longer referenced), added `ModalHeader` to `@patternfly/react-core` import.

### Fix 3 — Button icon children to prop
**No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances (lines 334 and 337) contain only text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Save')}`), not icon elements. The icon-to-prop migration only applies when Buttons contain icon components from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

### Fix 4 — HelperTextItem icon children to prop
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` (line 261) contains only text children (`{t('The name must be accurate...')}`), not an icon element. The icon-to-prop migration only applies when HelperTextItem contains icon components.

1. **Line 3**: `ModalHeader` removed from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` import (no longer used after removing the `Header` variable)
2. **Line 16**: `ModalHeader` added to `@patternfly/react-core` import (PF v6's ModalHeader)
3. **Lines 167-169** (old): `Header` variable removed (was only used by the removed `header` prop)
4. **Lines 169-174**: Modal props cleaned up — `header={Header}`, `showClose={false}`, `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}` all removed
5. **Line 175**: PF v6 `<ModalHeader title={...} />` added as first child of `<Modal>`
6. **Lines 334, 339**: `variant="secondary"` and `variant="primary"` — unchanged (still valid in v6)
7. **Lines 334-345**: Buttons contain only text, no icons — no icon migration needed
8. **Line 261**: `<HelperTextItem>` contains only text, no icon — no icon migration needed

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — Button variant type change
**No change needed.** The values `"secondary"` (line 334) and `"primary"` (line 339) are both still valid in the new type union. The only change was the addition of `"stateful"` — no existing values were removed.

### Fix 2 — Modal family migration
**Applied.** Three changes made:
1. **Removed `header={Header}` prop** from `<Modal>` and replaced with `<ModalHeader title={t('Key Management Service Advanced Settings')} />` as a direct child of `<Modal>` (PF v6 composition pattern).
2. **Removed `showClose={false}` prop** — no longer exists in PF v6 Modal API (close button is controlled by `onClose` presence on `<Modal>`).
3. **Removed `hasNoBodyWrapper={true}` prop** — no longer exists in PF v6 Modal API.
4. **Removed the `Header` variable** (was `const Header = <ModalHeader>...</ModalHeader>`) — now unused after removing the `header` prop.
5. **Updated imports**: Removed `ModalHeader` from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` (no longer referenced), added `ModalHeader` to `@patternfly/react-core` import.

### Fix 3 — Button icon children to prop
**No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances (lines 334 and 337) contain only text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Save')}`), not icon elements. The icon-to-prop migration only applies when Buttons contain icon components from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

### Fix 4 — HelperTextItem icon children to prop
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` (line 261) contains only text children (`{t('The name must be accurate...')}`), not an icon element. The icon-to-prop migration only applies when HelperTextItem contains icon components.
